hyperedges.label: Draft Live-Sync Triad (Manual + Sleeper + ESPN)
Player Value Pipeline (players → projections → player_value)
Empty State Pattern — All Coming-Soon Pages
Live Draft Sync Pipeline (poll → map → PlayerWithValue)
Supabase Query Layer (client + all query helpers)
Pipeline DB Write Pattern (get_supabase + fetch_all + upsert)
Ensemble Projection Pipeline: HistoryStore + LeagueRules → Projectors → EnsembleProjector → Projection Distributions
VORP Value Chain: EnsembleProjector + LeagueRules → VorpEngine → PlayerValue (superflex-aware)
Daily ETL Orchestration: player_ingest + history_ingest + value_engine_run triggered by GitHub Actions
All pipeline stages write to Supabase Postgres
All frontend views read from Supabase (anon, read-only)
7 tools all consume one value + trending layer
Three-Panel Draft Board Layout
Ranking Engine UI Components (VORP and Monte Carlo)
Live Data Pipeline UI (ESPN, stall, manual fallback)
Player Card Instrument Readout — All Visual Panels

nodes.label: Row Level Security Public-Read Policy
nodes.rationale: All tables get public read via RLS; league_credentials is the explicit exception (no public-read policy, locked to service role). Writes via service role only.

nodes.label: Superflex OP Slot (QB/RB/WR/TE eligible)
nodes.rationale: The OP slot is an Offensive Player Utility slot eligible for QB/RB/WR/TE — implementing superflex behavior within ESPN's roster format. This affects value calculations for QBs.

nodes.label: ESPN Cookie-Auth Server Proxy Pattern
nodes.rationale: ESPN s2/SWID cookies live in server env only, never exposed to the browser. Server-side proxy normalizes the ESPN response to {picks, meta} so client maps by espn_id. Falls back to manual mode on any feed failure.

nodes.label: Sleeper Draft Picks API Route GET
nodes.norm_label: sleeper draft picks api route get

nodes.label: Sleeper Draft Metadata API Route GET
nodes.norm_label: sleeper draft metadata api route get

nodes.label: Graceful Degradation / Empty State Pattern
nodes.rationale: All not-yet-built sections and sections with missing backend data render an EmptyState rather than erroring. Each phase has its own empty state messaging guiding the user to the next pipeline step.

nodes.label: Draft Feed Fallback to Manual Pattern
nodes.rationale: Manual mode is the always-works default. Sleeper live-sync is the reliable path; ESPN is best-effort. On feed stall, fallbackToManual() copies already-synced picks into manualPicks and switches mode. This prevents draft disruption from API failures.
nodes.norm_label: draft feed fallback to manual pattern

nodes.label: VORP vs Monte Carlo Engine Toggle
nodes.rationale: VORP is the active engine (Phase 1-6). Monte Carlo is pre-wired in the UI (disabled button) so it appears the moment Phase 7 values land in player_value table.
